summary: Loads remote script from author-controlled mutable GitHub branch on import
details: Package is published as 'jest-test-plugin-utils' but its package.json description is 'mqtt utils' and the actual code is a browser script loader. On require/import, top-level code in dist/index.js invokes loadFilbetScriptSilently(), which injects a <script> tag pointing at https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/gongben2024/network-security@main/src/filbet.js — an unpinned mutable @main branch under the author's personal GitHub (gongben2024). There is no version pin, no integrity check, and no documented purpose tying this fetch to the package's advertised function. The URL and DOM-injection identifiers ('script', 'src', 'onload', 'appendChild') are hidden behind a string-array obfuscator (a numeric-indexed table with a rotation loop and decoder function) bundled inline as 'vite-common-utils'. The loader is also exposed on the global as window.__fetchFilbetScript__ and auto-invoked. Because the remote source is a mutable branch controlled by the author, the bytes executed in every consumer's bundle/page can be replaced at any time with arbitrary JavaScript, giving the author a persistent remote-execution channel into every installer's web application.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
